# Copilot / AI agent instructions for CleanCooking_os

Purpose: give AI coding agents focused, actionable knowledge so edits are correct and fast.

Short contract
- Inputs: code changes, new/edited TSV inputs in data/inputs, and config.yaml in repo root.
- Outputs: code edits, tests, or documentation changes that keep existing CLI/Excel workflows working.
- Error modes: missing TSVs (routes), malformed `config.yaml`, or running in the wrong `interactive_mode`.

Quick dev run (Windows cmd.exe)
- Create venv and install deps:
  python -m venv .venv
  .venv\Scripts\activate
  pip install -r requirements.txt
- Run the model in-place (developer):
  python main.py

Where to look first (high value files)
- `main.py` — bootstraps logging, loads `src.config`, builds DataManager/PreprocessManager and runs `SimulationRunner`.
- `src/__init__.py` — loads `config.yaml` from the repo parent; the package-level `config` object is used everywhere.
- `src/route_configuration_loader.py` — Route TSV format: header must include Description, Folder, File; it resolves paths into `resolved_files` used by `DataManager`.
- `src/base_file_loader.py` — canonical TSV loader and `clean_data()` behaviour: tab-delimited, UTF-8, drops Unnamed columns, trims strings and empty rows.
- `src/data_manager.py` — holds loaded DataFrames in `.dataframes` and auto-detects relations based on column names.
- `src/preprocess_manager.py` — calls `Technologies` and `DemandAreaManager` to produce preprocessed outputs.
- `src/simulation_runner.py` — core pipeline: EL (electricity) phase, LPG phase, rest fuels, and final financial aggregation. Use it to reason about state changes.

Project-specific patterns and conventions
- TSV-first: input files are tab-separated .tsv and often produced by the Excel front-end. Keep `BaseFileLoader` behaviour when adding new loaders.
- Three-tier configuration: `config.yaml` (root) -> `RouteConfigurationLoader` (routes TSV) -> per-file loaders. `config` object is imported via `from src import config`.
- Interactive vs Offline modes: toggle `interactive_mode` in `config.yaml`.
  - interactive_mode: true -> expects preprocessed files in `data/offline_data_files` and skips expensive preprocessing.
  - false -> runs full preprocessing and writes into `data/offline_data_files` and `data/outputs`.
- Naming conventions: DataFrames are registered by name (e.g. `enriched_technologies`, `Territory_partition`) and accessed with `DataManager.get_dataframe(name)`.
- Simulation step APIs: many domain classes expose `run_simulation()` or `run_deployment()` — prefer calling those rather than reimplementing the internal sequencing.
- Caching: `SimulationRunner.ResultCache` caches Adoption/Income instances by (state, demand_area, tech_key) — preserve or adapt cache keys if changing model inputs.

Integration and outputs
- Logs: `log/app.txt` (configured in LogHandler). Use logging to trace changes.
- HTML report: generated by `ReportGenerator.generate_html_report(...)` (called in `main.py` finally block) and saved to `report/`.
- Outputs: `data/summary_results/` (quick checks) and `data/outputs/` (detailed results). Do not assume fields — inspect produced TSV headers when changing writers.

When making edits
- If changing file formats, update `src/base_file_loader.py` and `src/route_configuration_loader.py` accordingly and add tests reading example TSVs from `data/inputs/`.
- Prefer small, focused commits that update: loader -> preprocess -> simulation order.
- Keep public function names and `run_simulation()` style APIs to minimize ripple changes.

Examples (explicit references)
- To add a new input table called `MyTable.tsv`: add its route to the routes TSV used by `RouteConfigurationLoader` (Description/Folder/File), then ensure consumers access it via `DataManager.get_dataframe('MyTable')`.
- To change how technologies are computed: edit `src/technologies.py` and validate via `PreprocessDataManager._calculate_technologies()` and re-run offline mode.

Questions to ask the user if unclear
- Where is `config.yaml` for the run you want reproduced (Excel-generated vs repo root)?
- Should changes preserve the Excel/GUI front-end compat (Y/N)?

If you want, I can iterate this doc with more examples (TSV snippets, common failure traces) — tell me which parts you want expanded.
